source: NvChad - Marksman: what Marksman is, installing it via Mason and wiring it into lspconfig

options: A language server (LSP) that assists writing/maintaining Markdown
questions.why: Marksman is an LSP for Markdown.

options: Completion, go-to definition, reference search, rename refactoring, diagnostics
questions.why: Typical LSP features, applied to Markdown documents.

questions.q: Which command installs Marksman from Mason?
To activate Marksman you add it to the servers list in which file?
questions.why: Add "marksman" to local servers in configs/lspconfig.lua.

questions.q: Which plugin integrates language servers into NvChad?
questions.why: nvim-lspconfig supplies the configs for the servers.

questions.q: Which command shows the language servers applied to the current buffer?
questions.why: :LspInfo lists clients, filetypes, root directory and cmd.

questions.q: Why is the LSP 'root directory' (working directory) important?
options: The server needs a global view of the project (links, images)
questions.why: The root folder is like a 'Project' in graphical IDEs.

questions.q: Which commands show and change the working directory for a buffer?
options: :pwd shows it; :lcd changes it for that buffer only
questions.why: :pwd prints it; :lcd reassigns it only for that buffer.
